name: Gallium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Gallium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Gallium oxide removal without excessive melting

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for moderate Gallium absorption with good penetration

spotSize.description: Optimal spot diameter for precise cleaning of Gallium surfaces

repetitionRate.description: Balanced repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Minimum fluence required for effective Gallium oxide removal

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulses for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Scan speed for efficient area coverage while maintaining cleaning effectiveness

passCount.description: Number of passes for complete contaminant removal without substrate damage

overlapRatio.description: Optimal overlap between adjacent laser tracks for uniform cleaning

power.description: Typical range for pulsed Nd:YAG laser cleaning of soft metals; conservative max to avoid melting Gallium (mp 29.8°C, high reflectivity ~70-90% at 1064 nm reduces absorption); starting at 50 W for light contamination removal without substrate damage. Source: Generalized from metal cleaning studies (e.g., Laser Cleaning of Metals, SPIE proceedings); specific Ga data limited, conservative estimate.

scan_speed.description: Range for effective contaminant ablation on metals; slower for heavy contamination (200-500 mm/s), faster for light (1000-3000 mm/s) to minimize heat input on thermally conductive Gallium (40.6 W/m·K). Recommended 1000 mm/s for optimal balance. Source: Industry standards from IPG Photonics and Coherent manuals for 1064 nm systems; adapted for low-melt metals.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for Gallium to ablate contaminants via photothermal effects while minimizing heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond/femtosecond for ultra-precise but less common industrially due to cost. Value 20 ns balances efficiency and low thermal damage. Source: Laser physics research on metal ablation (e.g., Appl. Phys. A, 2015 on ns/ps cleaning); conservative for Ga's low mp.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for industrial cleaning to achieve high throughput; 20 kHz recommended for Gallium to avoid heat accumulation (high freq increases overlap heating, risky for low mp); lower rates for heavy contamination. Trade-off: higher rate speeds process but risks melting. Source: Academic literature (e.g., J. Laser Appl., 2018 on pulse train effects in metals); generalized for Ga.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal on reflective metals; Ga damage threshold estimated ~1-2 J/cm² (based on ablation studies of similar metals, adjusted for high reflectivity and low mp); 0.5 J/cm² recommended to stay below threshold. Source: Ablation threshold studies (e.g., Opt. Lasers Eng., 2020 on Al/Ga alloys); uncertainty noted for pure Ga, conservative values used.

spot_size.description: Typical beam diameter for industrial cleaning; smaller (200-400 μm) for fine details on Ga surfaces (e.g., electronics prep), larger (500-800 μm) for broad areas. 500 μm recommended for uniform coverage without excessive heat concentration. Source: Equipment specs from Trumpf and CleanLaser systems for 1064 nm cleaners; adapted for delicate substrates like Ga.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-4) for heavy oxides on Gallium; 2 passes recommended for complete removal while limiting total energy input to prevent melting. Source: Industry case studies on metal surface prep (e.g., Proc. SPIE, 2019); practical for Ga based on low thermal tolerance, uncertainty in specific Ga trials.

overlap_ratio.description: Standard range for uniform scan coverage in laser cleaning; 50% recommended for Gallium to ensure even contaminant removal without gaps or excessive overlap heating (high conductivity aids dissipation but low mp limits intensity). Source: Process optimization studies (e.g., J. Clean. Prod., 2021 on scan strategies); typical 30-80% adjusted conservatively for metals.
